name: Haris Khan
headline: Computer Science Graduate | Research Assistant
summary: I am a Computer Science graduate and Research Assistant with experience in applied artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, and research-oriented software development. My work spans model development, dataset preparation, experimental evaluation, technical writing, mentoring, and applied prototypes built through research projects and international hackathons.
contactText: I welcome conversations about research collaboration, academic projects, mentoring, and technical work in Computer Science.

bullets: Final Year Project: Outdoor Object Detection System for the Visually Impaired.
Built a YOLOv8-based real-time detection and audio guidance system using Python, OpenCV, and Google Text-to-Speech.
Worked on custom dataset preparation, training, evaluation, and application integration.
Contribute to research projects involving machine learning, deep learning, and computer vision.
Clean, preprocess, and organize datasets for model training and evaluation.
Train and fine-tune deep learning models, including YOLO-based architectures.
Assist with methodology development, experimental analysis, and research writing.

publications.title: Fruits Plant Leaf Diseases Detection using YOLOv12 Model with Flash Attention Mechanism and R-ELAN Block
publications.authors: I. Ahmad, M. Haris Khan, A. Khan, D. Ramli, M. Asim, M. Isha, and A. Khan

achievements: Top 5% globally and 8th nationally in Meta Hacker Cup 2025. View
Selected as a Judge for Student HackPad 2025, an international hackathon with 800+ participants and $14K+ in prizes. View
Led a team at Harvard CS50x Puzzle Day 2025, solving 8/9 puzzles. View
Ranked 3rd at HackHub 2025 by SparkHub for Exam Companion, an AI-powered exam planner. View
Ranked in the Top 10% globally at the International Computer Science Competition. View
Ranked 177th out of 355 in the MIT Informatics Contest. View
Achieved a TOEFL score of 91, equivalent to IELTS Band 7.0. View
teachingService: Trainer and Moderator at ICodeGuru, teaching data structures and LeetCode problem-solving patterns to 50+ underprivileged students. View
Led a 5-week IELTS preparation course focused on speaking, writing, and structured feedback. View
Delivered workshop: Transformers Architecture — From Attention to LLMs. View
Delivered workshop: Applied Computer Vision with YOLO — Detection and Segmentation. View
Delivered workshop: Exploring NASA Space Apps Challenge 2025. View
Served as a Relief Distribution Volunteer with Alkhidmat Foundation. View

mentorship.title: Judge and Mentor — HEC Generative AI Training Hackathon
mentorship.description: Mentored teams on idea development, technical feasibility, implementation quality, and presentation clarity for Generative AI projects.

mentorship.title: Judge — Student HackPad 2025
mentorship.description: Reviewed student-led educational technology projects and provided structured feedback on innovation, usability, learning impact, and implementation.

mentorship.title: Judge — HealTech Innovators Hackathon 2025
mentorship.description: Evaluated AI-driven healthcare ideas for technical quality, feasibility, clarity, and real-world applicability in health-focused settings.
